title: fumaria densiflora subsp. bracteosa
intro: Fumaria densiflora subsp. bracteosa, commonly known as Rock Whitlow-grass, is a flowering plant species in the family Fumariaceae. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Fumaria densiflora subsp. bracteosa include its potential in treating various conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
